title: The Kingdom of God Is Within You
description: Tolstoy's Christian anarchist critique of church, state, violence, and coercion.
descriptionParagraphs: Tolstoy's Christian anarchist critique of church, state, violence, and coercion.

The Kingdom of God Is Within You is usually read through its treatment of pacifism, religion, and anti state. As a religious and political treatise, it turns those concerns into conflicts of character, voice, setting, and social pressure rather than leaving them as abstract ideas.

Part of the work's durability lies in the way its form intensifies its themes. Readers return to it not only for subject matter but for the distinctive voice, structure, and atmosphere through which it makes pacifism, religion, and anti state feel immediate.

cover.alt: The Kingdom of God Is Within You cover thumbnail from Wikipedia

overview.englishDescription: Tolstoy's Christian anarchist critique of church, state, violence, and coercion.
banningParagraphs: The Kingdom of God Is Within You entered censorship debates as a religious and political treatise associated with pacifism, religion, and anti state. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around religious dissent and anti state.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1890s in Russia, where Tsarist censors banned publication. Tolstoy's fusion of radical Christianity and anti-state ethics alarmed imperial censors. The ban reflects both political and ecclesiastical hostility to Tolstoyism.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: Assassins of the Mind
counterReadings.author: Christopher Hitchens
counterReadings.note: Frames the Rushdie affair as a test of free speech against violent religious intimidation.

counterReadings.title: From Fatwa to Jihad
counterReadings.author: Kenan Malik
counterReadings.note: Tracks how conflicts over blasphemy, race, and offense evolved after the Rushdie controversy.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.
